---
title: "Random slopes"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source('../assets/setup.R')
```

Random slopes over some predictor `p` are

- the way the model adjusts the average ([fixed](fixef.html)) slope over `p` to better match the data from each level of the [grouping variable(s)](groupstruc.html).
- one number per level of the grouping variable, which is added on to the fixed slope over `p` to produce the group-specific slope over `p`.
- also called "varying slopes" or "slope adjustments".
- represented by the `+ p` in the random effect `lme4` syntax: `(1 + p | g)`, where `g` is the grouping variable.

To work out whether your model can contain random slopes over any of its predictors, see [Identify possible random effects](identify-ranef.html).



## Linked flash cards

### Outgoing links

- TODO


### Backlinks

- TODO
